lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਉ ਰਾਖਤ ਦੇ ਕਰਿ ਹਾਥ ॥
lines.translit: taa kau raakhat de kar haath |
lines.english: He preserves them, and holds out His Hands to protect them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੈ ਨ ਰਾਖੈ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: maarai na raakhai avar na koe |
lines.english: No one else can kill or preserve
but these attempts are in vain.
You may make all sorts of efforts,
Meditate, O Nanak, on God, the invisible, the wonderful! ||5||
So why are you so anxious, O mortal?
He is the Protector of all beings.
Time after time, again and again, meditate on God.
The jewel of the Naam is obtained by the Gurmukhs;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੀ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਧ੍ਰਪੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: pee amrit ihu man tan dhrapeeai |
lines.english: Drinking in this Nectar, this mind and body are satisfied.
they see no other than God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੋ ਸੁਖੁ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਕਾ ਸੰਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: naamo sukh har naam kaa sang |
lines.english: The Naam is peace, the Lord's Name is their companion.
Unto them, the Naam is wealth, the Naam is beauty and delight.
their minds and bodies are drenched with the Naam.
While standing up, sitting down and sleeping, the Naam,
Those who are satisfied by the essence of the Naam

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਜਨ ਕੈ ਸਦ ਕਾਮ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak jan kai sad kaam |6|
lines.english: says Nanak, is forever the occupation of God's humble servant. ||6||
God Himself has given this gift to His servants.
Chant His Praises with your tongue, day and night.
They recognize God's Own Command.
they remain absorbed in God Himself.
They know the past and the present.
Performing devotional worship with heart-felt love,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕਾ ਗੁਨੁ ਕਹਿ ਏਕ ਨ ਜਾਨਉ ॥
lines.translit: tis kaa gun keh ek na jaanau |
lines.english: I cannot describe even one of His virtuous qualities.
Those who dwell in God's Presence, twenty-four hours a day
Who can describe His Glory?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਮੇਰੇ ਤਿਨ ਕੀ ਓਟ ਲੇਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: man mere tin kee ott lehi |
lines.english: O my mind, seek their protection;

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਅਪਨਾ ਤਿਨ ਜਨ ਦੇਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: man tan apanaa tin jan dehi |
lines.english: give your mind and body to those humble beings.
- says Nanak, they are the perfect persons. ||7||
Those humble beings who recognizes God

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕੀ ਸਰਨਿ ਸਰਬ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tis kee saran sarab sukh paaveh |
lines.english: In His Sanctuary, all comforts are obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਸਰਬ ਥੋਕ ਕਾ ਦਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: so jan sarab thok kaa daataa |
lines.english: are the givers of all things.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਜਨ ਕੀ ਤੂ ਸੇਵਾ ਲਾਗੁ ॥
lines.translit: tis jan kee too sevaa laag |
lines.english: and enjoin yourself to the service of those servants.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕੈ ਦਰਸਿ ਸਭ ਪਾਪ ਮਿਟਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tis kai daras sabh paap mittaaveh |
lines.english: By the Blessing of His Darshan, all sins are erased.
So renounce all other clever devices,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਸੁ ਜਨ ਕੇ ਪੂਜਹੁ ਸਦ ਪੈਰਾ ॥੮॥੧੭॥
lines.translit: naanak tis jan ke poojahu sad pairaa |8|17|
lines.english: O Nanak, worship the feet of God's humble servants forever. ||8||17||
Salok:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਜਾਨਿਆ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਤਿਸ ਕਾ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sat purakh jin jaaniaa satigur tis kaa naau |
lines.english: The one who knows the True Lord God, is called the True Guru.
Your comings and goings shall be ended.
Ashtapadee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਸਿਖੁ ਉਧਰੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tis kai sang sikh udharai naanak har gun gaau |1|
lines.english: In His Company, the Sikh is saved, O Nanak, singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||1||
The True Guru cherishes His Sikh.
The Guru is always merciful to His servant.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਖ ਕੀ ਗੁਰੁ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਮਲੁ ਹਿਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: sikh kee gur duramat mal hirai |
lines.english: The Guru washes away the filth of the evil intellect of His Sikh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਿਖੁ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਤੇ ਹਾਟੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur kaa sikh bikaar te haattai |
lines.english: The Sikh of the Guru abstains from evil deeds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਿਖ ਕਉ ਨਾਮ ਧਨੁ ਦੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur sikh kau naam dhan dee |
lines.english: The True Guru gives His Sikh the wealth of the Naam.
Through the Guru's Teachings, he chants the Lord's Name.
The True Guru cuts away the bonds of His Sikh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਿਖ ਕਾ ਹਲਤੁ ਪਲਤੁ ਸਵਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: satigur sikh kaa halat palat savaarai |
lines.english: The True Guru arranges this world and the next for His Sikh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਿਖ ਕਉ ਜੀਅ ਨਾਲਿ ਸਮਾਰੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur sikh kau jeea naal samaarai |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, with the fullness of His heart, the True Guru mends His Sikh. ||1||
The Sikh of the Guru is very fortunate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਜੋ ਰਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai grihi sevak jo rahai |
lines.english: That selfless servant, who lives in the Guru's household,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਆਗਿਆ ਮਨ ਮਹਿ ਸਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: gur kee aagiaa man meh sahai |
lines.english: is to obey the Guru's Commands with all his mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਰਿਦੈ ਸਦ ਧਿਆਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: har har naam ridai sad dhiaavai |
lines.english: He is to meditate constantly within his heart on the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਸ ਕਉ ਕਰਿ ਕਛੁ ਨ ਜਨਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: aapas kau kar kachh na janaavai |
lines.english: He is not to call attention to himself in any way.
One who sells his mind to the True Guru
- that humble servant's affairs are resolved.
shall attain his Lord and Master.
One who performs selfless service, without thought of reward,
